title: Tether's USDT Jumps to 8.5% Premium in India After Crypto Payment Crackdown
summary: Tether's USDT stablecoin is trading at an 8.5% premium in India after raids on crypto payment firms in Bengaluru disrupted the pipeline that supplies USDT to Indian platforms. This is roughly double the usual gap, making USDT more expensive for Indian buyers.
body: Tether's USDT stablecoin is trading at an 8.5% premium in India following a crackdown on crypto payment firms in Bengaluru. The raids disrupted the pipeline that supplies USDT to Indian platforms, pushing its local price more than 8.5% above the dollar. This premium is roughly double the usual gap, making USDT more expensive for Indian buyers. The disruption underscores the fragility of crypto payment infrastructure in the country, especially for stablecoins like USDT, which are pegged to the US dollar.
twitterThread: Tether's USDT is now trading at an 8.5% premium in India after raids on crypto payment firms in Bengaluru disrupted the supply pipeline. This is roughly double the usual gap, making USDT more expensive for Indian buyers. The disruption highlights the fragility of crypto payment infrastructure in the country.

The premium is a direct result of raids on crypto payment firms in Bengaluru, which disrupted the pipeline that feeds dollar-pegged USDT to Indian platforms. This makes it harder and more expensive for users to access stablecoins. For everyday users, this means higher costs for trading and transactions involving USDT.

If the premium persists, it may drive users to seek alternative stablecoins or payment methods. Watch for any regulatory updates or announcements from Tether and local exchanges that could affect USDT's availability and pricing.
